PROFILE

Kayoko Mimura

Seishinkai Headquarters, Seishinkan Dojo — 2nd-generation Director (Honorary Director)
1974.6.11 - 2015.11.27

Overview

After graduating from the English Department of Hokusei Gakuen Women's Junior College, she worked as an office employee while enrolling in the Seishin-ryu Karate Yomiuri Culture Class.
She became a 2nd-term trainee and was appointed Branch Chief of Sakaemachi, a post she held until 2006 (Heisei 18). In an interview with Dosports, the Hokkaido Shimbun's sports paper, she said her dream for the future was to become a "female Director."
In 2008 (Heisei 20), she underwent a full gastrectomy for stage 4 stomach cancer; that same year she became the first full-time staff member of the NPO Seishinkai.
In 2009 (Heisei 21), she was appointed Shihan.

Through daily training she became universally recognized as the President's top disciple and successor, and in 2012 (Heisei 24) she was appointed 2nd-generation Director of the Headquarters. Seishin-ryu Karate Renshi, 6th Dan.
She was a pioneer among women instructors in Seishinkai karate, becoming the organization's first female Shihan and first female Director.
Fluent in English and capable of simultaneous interpretation, she served as Head of the International Division, hosting the karate enthusiasts who visited from overseas every year. In 2013 (Heisei 25), she traveled to Sri Lanka to teach karate.
In 2015 (Heisei 27), she was awarded Kyoshi, 7th Dan. Alongside karate, she also trained in Okinawan kobudo (weapons arts) and iaido.
She served as Executive Director of the NPO All Japan Seishinkai and as Headquarters Director & Head of the International Division of the ISKF, laying the foundations of Seishinkai.

On November 27, 2015 (Heisei 27), her cancer recurred and she passed away suddenly at a young age. She was posthumously awarded the honorary rank of 9th Dan. More than 800 people, including members, attended her funeral.
Her contributions are honored with the title of Honorary Director.
In 2016 (Heisei 28), a memorial stone and a Seishinkai commemorative monument were erected at Fujino Seizan-en. A memorial visit to her grave is held three times each year — during Golden Week, Obon, and November.

Seishinkai

NPO All Japan Seishinkai Karate Federation

  • 2-1-27 Sakaedori, Shiroishi-ku, Sapporo
  • TEL 011-300-0048（Mon–Fri 13:00–16:00）
  • FAX 011-300-0094
  • Karate@seishinkan.ne.jp
